behaviors.description: The content passed to summary generation includes data from metrics endpoint, issue tracker API, and deployment log.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is cross-module handoff: three collectors must all feed the summarizer input (MOCK-MISS=yes for module interaction). This does not require real OS/network/subprocess if source adapters are replaced with in-process fakes (REAL-INFRA demoted). Correctness here is about inclusion/wiring, not model judgment quality (LLM-DEP=no). Not a multi-agent sequence (MULTI-STEP=no). Rejected System because behavior text is specifically about "content passed to the summary-generation step" rather than real endpoint/network behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the real orchestration path with three fake source clients returning distinct markers; execute collection+assembly; assert summarizer input payload contains all three sections/markers and none are dropped by handoff.

behaviors.description: If one source is unavailable, report is still produced with a "data unavailable" note for that section.
behaviors.justification: This is resilience/orchestration across modules: source failure must propagate into section-level fallback while pipeline continues (MOCK-MISS=yes at handoff/state propagation). In-process failure injection (exception/timeout from fake adapter) can reproduce required behavior, so real network/OS is not strictly required (REAL-INFRA demoted). No dependence on model output quality for pass/fail of fallback marker (LLM-DEP=no). No multi-agent chain (MULTI-STEP=no). Rejected System because behavior text targets fallback semantics ("data unavailable" note rather than aborting), not real transport-layer characteristics.
behaviors.test_description: Run orchestrator with two normal fake sources and one fake source that raises an availability error; assert output report is generated, failing section contains "data unavailable", and other sections remain populated.

behaviors.description: Narrative summary accurately represents key values in input data.
behaviors.justification: Pass/fail depends on the model’s generated content fidelity to inputs, which is an LLM output-quality check (LLM-DEP=yes). Deterministic stubs would hide hallucination/omission errors (MOCK-HIDE=yes). No real OS/network boundary is the primary failure boundary beyond the model call itself. Rejected Workflow because behavior text mentions a single summary-generation decision ("narrative summary") and does not describe multiple agentic stages in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run summary generation against real model calls on a fixed dataset with known key values; evaluate whether output preserves required facts (values/trends) and does not invent conflicting numbers, using rubric/LLM-judge with repeated runs and thresholded pass criteria.

behaviors.description: Complete report flow (collection through email delivery) reaches configured recipient inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary includes real external delivery path and inbox receipt verification (REAL-INFRA=yes: real email service/network). Mock mailers can pass while masking auth, TLS, provider rejection, or routing issues (MOCK-HIDE=yes). This behavior does not require judging LLM output quality; success criterion is delivery to inbox (LLM-DEP not required). Rejected Agentic because behavior text is "reaches ... inbox correctly," not "summary quality is correct." Rejected Workflow because no multi-agent decision pipeline is asserted.
behaviors.test_description: Execute script in an environment with real integrations (or staging equivalents) and send to a test mailbox; assert message is received by configured recipient, with expected subject/body sections and attachments/format as applicable.

behaviors.description: If email service is unreachable, save report locally and retry delivery on next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly depends on real email-service unreachability semantics plus persistence across runs (REAL-INFRA=yes: real network failure modes; scheduler/run-boundary behavior). Pure mocks can miss real client exception shapes and reconnect/retry edge cases (MOCK-HIDE=yes). No model-output-quality dependency (LLM-DEP=no). Not a multi-agent chain (MULTI-STEP=no). Rejected Workflow because behavior text describes operational retry across runs, not multiple agentic reasoning steps.
behaviors.test_description: Run once with email endpoint intentionally unreachable in a real/staging network path; assert report file is written to local fallback storage. Run next scheduled invocation with service restored; assert persisted report is retried and successfully delivered, and retry state is cleared.
